title: PB-143 VERIFIED: GitHub Notion Euria Sync Architecture Review

premise_check.governance_effect: Treat the local branch evidence as authoritative for this review. Do not claim review of 49 commits because local Git evidence shows one unique commit.

commits_reviewed.title: governance(sync): define GitHub Notion Euria synchronization architecture
commits_reviewed.runtime_relevance: Defines synchronization authority boundaries but does not implement runtime execution.
commits_reviewed.automation_relevance: Defines future synchronization event rules but does not implement automation.

changed_files_inventory.production_relevance: Policy and architecture relevant only; not production executable.

executable_functionality.evidence: Branch diff adds one Markdown architecture document only.

synchronization_workflows.evidence: Event synchronization model is documented, but no workflow, script, or connector execution code is added.

github_integration.evidence: GitHub is documented as authoritative source and synchronization event origin.

notion_integration.evidence: Notion is documented as navigation and coordination only.

euria_integration.evidence: Euria is documented as operational validation layer only.

usbay_control_plane_integration.evidence: No USBAY Control Plane code, API route, runtime status path, or dashboard integration is added.

unique_capabilities_introduced.description: Defines GitHub as the source of truth for governance policies, architecture decisions, certification decisions, blocker registers, evidence packages, audit dossiers, validation scripts, tests, and runtime implementation references.
Defines Notion as a coordination and navigation layer that may link and summarize but may not override GitHub, close blockers, host authoritative evidence, or create certification claims.
Defines Euria as an operational review and validation layer that may draft evidence-bound notes but may not override GitHub or invent missing evidence.

Defines BLOCKED outcomes for missing GitHub evidence, GitHub/Notion disagreement, GitHub/Euria disagreement, missing source links, missing audit metadata, missing evidence packages, and certification or blocker state asserted outside GitHub.
Defines required event type, actor, timestamp, source system, target system, GitHub source path, commit SHA, evidence reference, decision impact, and validation status.

what_is_implemented: A documented synchronization architecture between GitHub, Notion, and Euria.
what_is_documentation_only: All branch changes are documentation-only in the committed diff.
what_is_production_relevant: The authority boundary is production-relevant as a governance policy reference.
The fail-closed disagreement model is production-relevant as design input.
what_advances_usbay_automation: It defines the governance contract future automation must follow.
what_should_be_merged_first: The architecture document may be considered first because it is scoped, documentation-only, and establishes authority boundaries needed before implementation.
what_should_be_deferred: Any USBAY Control Plane runtime coordination.

final_answers.what_is_documentation_only: The entire committed branch diff is documentation-only.
final_answers.what_is_production_relevant: The authority and fail-closed boundaries are production-relevant as governance design input.
final_answers.what_advances_usbay_automation: The branch defines the governance rules future automation must obey, but implements no automation.
final_answers.what_should_be_merged_first: If reviewers accept the full-suite validation gap as unrelated to this docs-only branch, merge the architecture document first as a foundation. Under strict fail-closed policy, do not merge until full validation is clean or the validation gap is explicitly waived by governance.
final_answers.what_should_be_deferred: Live connector execution, external API calls, Notion/Euria mutation, GitHub mutation automation, and USBAY Control Plane runtime coordination.
